Factory Act Compliance

End-to-end management of the Factories Act. We secure factory licences, manage renewals, ensure safety protocol compliance, and handle statutory returns across India.

Overview

Built for the Realities of Factory Compliance

The Factories Act, 1948 governs the health, safety, and welfare of workers in manufacturing establishments. Our comprehensive Factory Act services ensure your factory operations remain compliant with all statutory requirements while maintaining a safe working environment.

Our Factory Act practice sits inside a broader compliance advisory, so licence management, safety protocols, and statutory returns are handled alongside the rest of your HR obligations, not as standalone filing exercises.

Our Services

Factories Act Management Services

Our services cover the full spectrum of Factory Act compliance, from licensing to safety protocols.

  • Factory licence acquisition and renewals
  • Safety protocol implementation and compliance
  • Statutory returns filing and record maintenance
  • Health and welfare facility management
  • Inspection support and compliance audits
  • Plan approval and modification support
Safety-First Compliance

Safety-First Compliance

We believe that Factory Act compliance is fundamentally about worker safety. Our approach integrates legal compliance with practical safety measures.

Safety Policy

Safety policy development and implementation.

Risk Assessment

Hazard identification and risk assessment.

Safety Equipment

Safety equipment specification and compliance.

Emergency Planning

Emergency preparedness planning.

Safety Training

Safety training and awareness programs.

Inspection Support

Inspection handling and compliance audit support.

“

Factory Act compliance errors rarely stay a paperwork issue for long — non-compliance can lead to license cancellations, regulatory penalties, and tend to compound over time as operational risk accumulates.

— Czar Consultancy, Factory Act Compliance
Health & Welfare

Health & Welfare Compliance

The Factories Act mandates specific health and welfare provisions for factory workers. Our team ensures all health and welfare obligations are met, including medical facilities, drinking water, sanitation, and other statutory requirements.

  • Medical facility compliance and management
  • Drinking water and sanitation standards
  • Rest room and canteen facility compliance
  • Lighting, ventilation, and temperature standards
  • Welfare facility audits and improvements
How It Works

Our Compliance Process

02

Safety Implementation

Ongoing Support

Continuous support for renewals and inspections.

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the Factories Act, 1948?+
The Factories Act, 1948 is a comprehensive legislation enacted by the Parliament of India to regulate the conditions of work in factories. It governs the health, safety, and welfare of workers in manufacturing establishments. The Act applies to factories employing 10 or more workers with power, or 20 or more workers without power.
Who needs to obtain a factory licence?+
Any factory employing 10 or more workers with power, or 20 or more workers without power, must obtain a factory licence. The licence is issued by the Chief Inspector of Factories and must be renewed periodically. The specific requirements vary based on the type of manufacturing process and the number of workers.
What are the safety requirements under the Factories Act?+
The Factories Act mandates specific safety requirements including fencing of machinery, safety devices for dangerous operations, protective equipment for workers, and safe working conditions. It also requires regular safety inspections, maintenance of safety records, and implementation of safety protocols to prevent accidents.
What statutory returns need to be filed?+
Factories must file various statutory returns including annual returns, returns related to accidents and dangerous occurrences, returns related to workers' health and welfare, and returns related to safety inspections. The frequency and format of these returns vary by state and the type of manufacturing activity.
How often do factory licences need to be renewed?+
Factory licences typically need to be renewed annually. The exact timeline varies by state, but most states require renewal within 30 days of the licence expiry date. Our team tracks all renewal deadlines and ensures timely filing to maintain continuous compliance.
